description.content: Operations for retrieving add-on definitions that can be applied to subscriptions to increase their price.

Returns all add-on definitions configured for the merchant account. Add-ons represent incremental price increases that can be applied to subscriptions on top of the base plan price. Add-ons are defined in the Braintree Control Panel and referenced by their identifier when creating or updating subscriptions.

variable.description: The unique identifier for the merchant account.

response.name: List of add-on definitions retrieved successfully.

variable.description: The unique identifier for the merchant account.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: basic
Operations for retrieving discount definitions that can be applied to subscriptions to reduce their price.

Returns all discount definitions configured for the merchant account. Discounts represent price reductions that can be applied to subscriptions to decrease the amount charged each billing cycle. Discounts are defined in the Braintree Control Panel and referenced by their identifier when creating or updating subscriptions.

variable.description: The unique identifier for the merchant account.

response.name: List of discount definitions retrieved successfully.

variable.description: The unique identifier for the merchant account.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: basic
Operations for retrieving billing plan definitions configured in the Braintree Control Panel.

Returns all recurring billing plans configured for the merchant account in the Braintree Control Panel. Plans define the billing cycle frequency, base price, currency, and optional trial period for subscriptions. Plans must be created through the Control Panel and cannot be created via the API.

variable.description: The unique identifier for the merchant account.

response.name: List of billing plans retrieved successfully.

variable.description: The unique identifier for the merchant account.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: basic
Operations for creating, retrieving, updating, canceling, and retrying customer subscriptions to recurring billing plans.

Creates a new recurring billing subscription for a customer. Requires a plan_id identifying the billing plan and either a payment_method_token or payment_method_nonce identifying the payment method to charge each billing cycle. The subscription begins immediately or on a specified future date, optionally with a trial period. Add-ons and discounts from the plan can be inherited, overridden, or supplemented with new ones.

variable.description: The unique identifier for the merchant account.

response.name: Subscription created successfully.

Updates an existing subscription. Supports changing the payment method token, price, plan, billing day, number of billing cycles, and add-ons or discounts. Price changes take effect immediately with prorated billing applied for the current cycle. Changing the plan can optionally restart the billing cycle.

variable.description: (Required) The unique identifier of the subscription.
The unique identifier for the merchant account.

Cancels an active subscription immediately. The subscription status transitions to Canceled and no further billing cycles will be charged. Cancellation is a terminal state and cannot be reversed; a new subscription must be created to resume billing.

The unique identifier for the merchant account.

Manually retries a failed subscription charge for a subscription in the Past Due status. An optional amount may be specified to charge a different amount than the current billing cycle amount. This is useful for resolving payment failures without waiting for the automatic retry schedule.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: basic
The unique identifier for the merchant account.

The Braintree Subscriptions API enables merchants to manage recurring billing plans and customer subscriptions. Merchants define billing plans with configurable pricing, billing cycles, and trial periods, then subscribe customers using vaulted payment method tokens. The API handles automatic charge retries, prorated billing for mid-cycle changes, add-ons and discounts, and subscription lifecycle events. Subscription status changes are communicated via webhook notifications. Authentication uses HTTP Basic auth with the merchant's public key as the username and private key as the password.
